name: KeyCorp

profile.description: KeyCorp is a bank-based financial services company with ~$185B in assets, operating primarily in the Midwest and Northeast U.S. through KeyBank National Association.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Steady banking operations. AI Deployment (1/2): AI for risk management and customer analytics. Market Share (1/2): Top-20 U.S. bank. Product Pipeline (0/2): No transformative products. R&D Intensity (1/1): Technology investment.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Standard community banking purpose. Community Impact (1/2): CRA lending and community development. Employee Retention (0/2): No standout data. Insider Buying (1/2): Some management buying.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored as Dividend. Forward P/E 10.09 excellent (1.0). ROE 9.98% (0.67). Revenue growth 11.8% (1.0). Profit margin 27.02% (1.0). D/E excluded for banks. Dividend: 25yr paid (2x), 10yr growth, 6% CAGR (2x), 48% payout.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): Banking AI disruption risk similar to FITB/C. Regulatory and relationship moats provide protection. AI Opportunity (1/1): AI deployment in operations.

infinite_game.summary: Solid regional bank with 25-year dividend track record but no distinctive infinite game characteristics.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Uses larger bank competition as motivation.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Adapted to digital banking.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Maintained dividend through cycles.

investment_thesis: KeyCorp at 10.09x forward P/E with 3.8% yield, 25 consecutive years of dividends, 10 years of growth, and 27% profit margin. EPS grew 33% YoY.
thesis_continued: ROE of 9.98% below target. Regional bank CRE exposure risk. Interest rate sensitivity. Competition from fintechs and larger banks.
key_risks: Commercial real estate exposure in potential downturn
sources: KeyCorp Q1 2026 Earnings — SEC Filings, Yahoo Finance, 2026
